# Apply a minimal pipeline via HCL, then drive it end-to-end through a real daemon:
# build (command) -> review (approval), checking Gate 1 (stage ordering) and RBAC
# along the way.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze identity register alice
cp stdout alice.token
exec breeze role assign reviewer alice --as admin --token-file admin.token

# --dry-run must never mutate: no pipeline registered yet, and it reports "new".
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token --dry-run
stdout '\+ pipeline release \(new\)'
exec breeze pipeline list --json
stdout '"pipelines":\s*null|"pipelines":\s*\[\]'

exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze pipeline show release --json
stdout '"name":\s*"release"'

# review can't be approved before build succeeds (Gate 1).
! exec breeze stage approve release review abc123 --as alice --token-file alice.token
stderr 'prerequisite'

exec breeze stage start release build abc123 --as ci
stdout 'build: succeeded'

exec breeze stage approve release review abc123 --as alice --token-file alice.token
stdout 'review: succeeded'

exec breeze pipeline status release abc123 --json
stdout '"stage":\s*"build"[\s\S]*"status":\s*"succeeded"'
stdout '"stage":\s*"review"[\s\S]*"status":\s*"succeeded"'

exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type    = "command"
    timeout = "10s"
    command = ["/bin/true", "{commit}"]
  }
  stage "review" {
    type               = "approval"
    required_approvals = 1
    approver_role      = "reviewer"
  }
}
